description: Fix resource read() examples in docs that cause lint errors when developers follow them. The resources-prompts guide shows `async read(uri: string)` in all code examples, but the scaffolded ESLint config (tseslint.configs.strict) flags unused parameters. Developers who copy the documented pattern and don't use the uri parameter get an immediate `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` lint error. The fix is to update all resource read() examples to omit the uri parameter when it is not used (TypeScript allows functions with fewer parameters to satisfy the interface), and add a brief note explaining the pattern for resources that serve a single URI vs. resources that dispatch on URI.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix resource read() examples to avoid unused-parameter lint errors
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the docs code examples for resource read() to not cause lint errors when I copy them, so that I don't get confused by immediate failures after following the tutorial.
acceptanceCriteria: All resource read() examples in resources-prompts.mdx that don't use the uri parameter omit it from the function signature (e.g., `async read()` instead of `async read(uri: string)`)
The single-URI resource example (channelList at ~line 29) uses `async read()` since it hardcodes the URI in the return value
The typed resource example (~line 125) uses `async read()` since it also hardcodes the URI
The full plugin example (~line 432) uses `async read()` for the same reason

A brief inline note is added near the first resource example explaining that the `uri` parameter can be omitted when the resource only serves one URI, and should be included when the read() function needs to dispatch based on the URI value
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx

The ResourceDefinition interface (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:~line 71) defines read as `read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>`. TypeScript structural typing allows a function with fewer parameters to satisfy this — so `async read(): Promise<ResourceContent>` is valid.

The scaffolded ESLint config uses `tseslint.configs.strict` which enables `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` strictly (no underscore-prefix exception). This means both `async read(uri: string)` and `async read(_uri: string)` produce lint errors if uri is unused.

Code examples to update:
1. ~line 35: `async read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>` → `async read(): Promise<ResourceContent>`
2. ~line 134: same pattern
3. ~line 432: same pattern in the full plugin example

Add a brief note after the first example (around line 47) like: 'The `uri` parameter can be omitted when the resource serves a single URI. Include it when your `read()` function needs to dispatch based on the URI value.'

Do NOT change the ResourceDefinition interface table or the return type docs — only the code examples and add the explanatory note.
